programmes.show_description: The Minister for Transport is on his way to visit the station, but disaster awaits as there is no milk left at the train's buffet for his cup of tea! S2 E1: No Milk For The Minister
When Gary discovers his photo in a biography of Noel Coward, he heads back in time to try and erase it from existence before Yvonne gets a chance to see it. S6 E9: Flash Bang Wallop
More mayhem with hapless bumbler Mr. Bean as he struggles to wait in line for an appointment and thinks up some unusual methods to help him sleep. E13: Goodnight Mr. Bean
A hostage situation becomes personal for Sam when the hostage-taker's deadline coincides with the deadline for switching off his life-support machine in 2006. Starring John Simm. S1 E6
It's Beryl's sister's wedding, but Dad is seeing another woman, Gloria doesn't want to get married, and Mum is putting on a brave face - time for Beryl and Sandra to help! S2 E1: The Wedding
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Where Malcolm takes Brenda to Chester, she plays second fiddle to a piece of furniture that he is returning for his mother. S1 E3: Outing
Dorien gets the idea that Sharon could be the heroine for her next book, and Tracey's got her hands full with a despondent Garth. S11 E2: There's Something About Sharon
Mr. Bean gets to an appointment with the dentist after oversleeping and has trouble in the park with a bothersome fly. E5: The Trouble with Mr. Bean
David refuses to go with Amy to visit the ruins by saying that he already booked a court to play tennis with Linda; Robert still does not suspect, but Amy finally sees the evidence. S1 E6: Forty Love

It is Boxing Day in the Pledge house, Bored Lily and Walter join Nellie and Eli in a seance. S3 E7: The Ghost of Picklers Past
With their affair now exposed, Linda and David plan to run off together - Robert wants to fight David, but Amy decides to foil their plan. S1 E7: Hasta La Vista
Lady Lavender has decided where her shares are going, and Ivy is in trouble for what she saw at the fancy dress party the night before. S1 E4 Fair Shares
Rigsby is dressed to kill as he takes Miss Jones out to dinner to celebrate her birthday. S1 E3: A Night Out
Feeling the stress of eviction and his dead-end place in the world after Shirley moves to Italy, Wolfie climbs up on the pub roof and threatens suicide. S3 E1: Don't Look Down

Reunited at last, Beryl and Sandra clash over their mothers when Beryl shatters Sandra's Rex-fuelled fantasy. Hurt and angry, Beryl moves out, threatening their fragile friendship. S10 E7: Moving Out
Jack is convinced that something is going on between May and Cecil - and with good reason as they knew each other 18 years earlier and May happens to have an 18-year-old daughter. S2 E2: Father's Day

It is Chrissy's birthday when she receives a cine camera and projector from her boyfriend Neil, but Larry plans to use it to watch blue movies. S5 E1: The Last Picture Show
All the students find themselves up court for causing breaches of the peace or riots. Flashbacks show how these came about. S3 E5: Guilty or Not Guilty
As Christmas approaches Alf Garnett's problems increase - but things are eventually resolved by Caribbean magic. In Sickness and in Health Christmas Special 1986

With the war coming to an end, Gary saves Clement Attlee's life but his future is foreclosed by a closed time portal. S6 E10: Accentuate the Positive
The blunderingly inept master of disaster goes for a haircut - but ends up styling the other customers' hair - and causes chaos at a school fun day. E14: Hair by Mr. Bean of London
Drama series about a detective who wakes up in what seems to be 1973. The death in custody of drug dealer Billy Kemble pits Sam against CID as he tries to uncover the truth. Starring John Simm. S1 E7

Sandra is devastated at splitting up with Peter Crawford, so Beryl decides to find him and bring him back to their flat - is it a case of mistaken identity when he arrives? S2 E2: Look Before You Leap

Sharon and Tracey discover that Dorien has been keeping a secret for over fifty years and decide to get involved, but Dorien is reluctant to confront her past. S11 E3: Guess Who's Coming to Essex?

Hit 70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S4, E1: A Price on Your Head
With Amy having sent all his clothes to the cleaners, David looks doomed to spend the rest of his holiday in drag and gets no sympathy from his wife or the Cochrans. S2 E1: Manana
Alf is being blackmailed and is determined to get his hands on Lady Lavender's shares, and Alf and Ivy's secret is revealed but to whom? S1 E5 Beg, Borrow or Steal
Rigsby is given some advice from Alan and Philip - as well as some tablets - to calm his nerves in his amorous pursuit of Miss Jones. S1 E4: Charisma

Hearing of plans to evict a gypsy from grassland in order to erect a squash court, Wolfie and the gang head to the town hall with plans to break into the planning meeting. S3 E2: Only Fools and Horses

George's car fails its MOT and he needs £20 to get it fixed - he sells his piano to Robin, but the problem is how to get it upstairs. S5 E2: Right Said George
Anna's au pair visa is set to expire, meaning she will have to leave England. The class decides the best way to get her to stay is to marry Anna off to an Englishman. S3 E6: Repent at Leisure
Alf's joy at Rita's pending divorce is short lived when he learns she is to live with the new doctor. S3 E1

The Winchester Club is faced with permanent closure when one of Arthur's money-making schemes backfires badly, but Ray has an idea, a charity football match. S9 E9: Last Orders at the Winchester
